sections.paragraph: com. (f. 1Ki 10:17); with suff. מָגִנִּי; pl. מָגִנִּים, const. מָגִנֵּי (prop. part. Hiph. from the root גָּנַן to protect), a shield, Jud. 5:8 2Sa 1:21 22:31 , 36 2Sa 22:36 2Ki 19:32, etc. (Arab. مِجَنُّ, مِجَنَّةُ id.) This word implies a shield of a smaller size and extent than עִנָּה; see 1Ki 10:16, 17 1Ki 10:17 2Ch 9:16 אִישׁ מָגֵן an armed man, used of a thief, Pro 6:11 24:34 . Metaph.-(a) of God as a protector, Gen 15:1 Psa 3:4 18:3 , 31 Psa 18:31 144:2 . Psa 7:11, מָגִנִּי עַל־אֱלהִים “my shield is with God,” i.e. God as it were holds my shield, protects me with a shield; compare Psa 89:19.-(b) מָגִנֵּי־אֶרֶץ “the shields of the land;” poet. for the princes, protecting the citizens with force of arms, Ps. 47:10 Hos 4:18.
